cases.passage: A five-member committee is to be formed from a group of 8 students and 4 teachers. If a student is to be the chairperson, find the probability that the committee will have at least 2 teachers. Assume that each member of the group is equally likely to be selected.

questions.q: How many ways can a student be selected as the chairperson?
questions.explanation: Since a student is to be the chairperson, we have only 1 option for this position.

questions.q: How many ways can a committee of 5 members be formed from 8 students and 4 teachers?
questions.explanation: The total number of ways to form a committee of 5 members from 12 people is 12C5.

questions.q: If a student is selected as the chairperson, what is the probability that the committee will have at least 2 teachers?
questions.explanation: The probability can be calculated using the formula P(at least 2 teachers) = P(2 teachers) + P(3 teachers) + P(4 teachers).

questions.q: What is the total number of committees that can be formed with a student as the chairperson?
questions.explanation: Since a student is to be the chairperson, we need to select 1 student out of 8 and 1 teacher out of 4.

questions.q: What is the probability that the committee will have no teachers if a student is selected as the chairperson?
questions.explanation: The probability can be calculated using the formula P(no teachers) = P(5 students).

cases.passage: A bag contains 5 red marbles, 8 blue marbles, and 4 green marbles. A marble is drawn at random from the bag. If the marble drawn is blue, find the probability that it was drawn from the first third of the bag.

questions.q: How many blue marbles are there in the bag?
What is the total number of marbles in the bag?
questions.explanation: The total number of marbles in the bag is 17.

questions.q: If a blue marble is drawn, what is the probability that it was drawn from the first third of the bag?
questions.explanation: The probability can be calculated using the formula P(first third) = P(blue marble in first third) / P(blue marble).

questions.q: What is the probability that a blue marble is drawn from the bag?
questions.explanation: The probability can be calculated using the formula P(blue marble) = (number of blue marbles) / (total number of marbles).

questions.q: What is the number of marbles in the first third of the bag?
questions.explanation: Since the bag contains 17 marbles, the first third of the bag contains 6 marbles.
